questions.question: Explain the concept of Remote Method Invocation (RMI) in Java.
keyPoints: RMI is a Java API for distributed computing
Allows objects in different JVMs to communicate
Provides transparency - remote objects appear local
Uses stubs and skeletons for communication

questions.question: What is the role of Stub and Skeleton in RMI? Explain.
keyPoints: Stub is client-side proxy representing remote object
Skeleton is server-side proxy receiving requests
Stub marshals requests, unmarshals responses
Skeleton unmarshals requests, marshals responses
They hide network communication from application

questions.question: Explain the purpose of RMI Registry in distributed applications.
keyPoints: Acts as naming service for remote objects
Allows registration of remote objects with names
Clients can lookup objects by name
Provides bootstrap namespace
Default port is 1099

questions.question: What is serialization in RMI and why is it important?
keyPoints: Serialization converts objects to byte streams
Used for passing parameters and return values
All parameters must be Serializable
Enables object transmission over network
Essential for marshalling/unmarshalling

questions.question: List the steps involved in developing an RMI application.
keyPoints: Define Remote Interface extending java.rmi.Remote
Implement Remote Class implementing the interface
Generate Stub and Skeleton using rmic tool
Start RMI Registry
Register remote object using Naming.bind()
Create client to lookup and use remote object

questions.question: Differentiate between RMI and CORBA.
keyPoints: RMI is Java-specific, CORBA is language-independent
RMI uses Java serialization, CORBA uses IDL
CORBA uses ORB, RMI doesn't
RMI simpler but limited to Java
CORBA more complex but cross-platform

questions.question: Explain the complete RMI architecture and communication flow with a neat diagram.
keyPoints: Explain three-layer architecture: Application, Stub/Skeleton, Transport
Describe step-by-step communication flow
Explain marshalling and unmarshalling process
Discuss role of RMI Registry
Explain how client locates remote object
Describe data flow from client to server and back
Mention serialization and network transport

questions.question: Write a complete Java program demonstrating RMI with a simple calculator service.
keyPoints: Define Remote Interface with calculator methods
Implement Remote Class with calculation logic
Create server to register remote object
Create client to lookup and invoke methods
Show proper exception handling
Include necessary imports and class definitions

questions.question: Discuss the advantages and limitations of using RMI for distributed application development.
keyPoints: Advantages: Object-oriented, type-safe, security, garbage collection, transparency
Limitations: Java-only, firewall issues, performance overhead, complex debugging
Comparison with other technologies
Use cases where RMI is appropriate
Discussion on dynamic class loading benefits
